About: It is about a Berber ethnonationalist movement.
Affects: It affects cultural Arabization, pan-Arabism, and Islamism.
It affects the Berber population in Algeria and Morocco.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with colonial capitalism.
It is associated with France's divide and conquer policy.
It is associated with the Kabyle myth.
It is associated with anti-Arab racism.
It is associated with Islamophobia.
It is associated with Francophilia.
It is associated with French colonial authorities.
It is associated with the Berber Dahir in Morocco.
It is associated with the Independence movement.
It is associated with the Movement for the Triumph of Democratic Liberties (MTLD).
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Maghreb region of North Africa.
CausedBy: It is caused by colonial capitalism.
It is caused by France's divide and conquer policy.
ConsistsOf: It consists of anti-Arab racism, Islamophobia, and Francophilia.
CreatedIn: It was created in Kabylia in Algeria.
CreatedTo: It was created to combat Arabism-Islamism.
It was created to combat colonialism.
HasA: It has separatist agendas aimed at secession from Maghrebi states.
It has demands in the political sphere.
It has a history of social unrest in Algerian politics.
It has a framework to combat colonialism.
It has a framework to combat Arabism-Islamism.
It has a call for recognition of Berber culture and language.
It has a call for a Western style democracy.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by anti-Arab racism.
It is characterized by Islamophobia.
It is characterized by Francophilia.
It is characterized by separatist agendas.
It is characterized by opposition to cultural Arabization.
It is characterized by opposition to pan-Arabism.
It is characterized by opposition to Islamism.
It is characterized by the Kabyle myth.
It is characterized by the Berber policy of the French colonial empire.
It is characterized by the Berber Spring.
Influenced: It influenced the Berber Cultural Movement (MCB).
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by colonial capitalism.
It was influenced by France's divide and conquer policy.
It was influenced by French colonial authorities.
It was influenced by colonial policies such as the Berber Dahir in Morocco.
It was influenced by the Kabyle myth.
It was influenced by the Berber policy of the French colonial empire.
It was influenced by the triumph of colonial capitalism in Kabylia.
It was influenced by French attempts at a Divide and Rule strategy in Algeria.
It was influenced by the 1962 Constitution.
It was influenced by Arabization measures.
IsA: It is a Berber ethnonationalist movement.
KnownFor: It is known for its opposition to Arabization.
It is known for its opposition to pan-Arabism.
It is known for its opposition to Islamism.
It is known for its pursuit of separatist agendas.
It is known for its role in the Berber Spring.
It is known for its call for a Western-style socio-political system.
It is known for its demand for recognition of Berber culture and language.
LocatedIn: It is located in Kabylia in Algeria.
It is located in the Maghreb region of North Africa.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the French colonial era.
It occurred during the period of French colonization.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the Maghreb region of North Africa.
OriginatedIn: It originated in Kabylia in Algeria.
UsedFor: It is used for the assertion of Berber culture.
It is used for the redefinition of society.
It is used for the implementation of a Western style democracy.
UsedTo: It is used to combat Arabism-Islamism.
It is used to combat colonialism.
It is used to oppose Arabization.
It is used to call for recognition of Berber culture and language.
It is used to implement a Western style democracy.
